subject: Biology
title: Why Cells Divide
description: A micro-lesson that makes Why Cells Divide usable.

hook.text: Your body makes about 3.8 million new cells every second to replace those that die.

buildup.text: Cells divide for growth, repair, and reproduction. Without division, wounds never heal.

discovery.text: As a cell grows, its surface can't keep up with its volume. It must split or starve.

twist.text: Some cells like neurons rarely divide after birth. Gut lining cells divide daily.

climax.text: Division is tightly controlled. Checkpoints ensure DNA is copied correctly before splitting.

punchline.text: Divide or die. Most cells choose divide.

quiz.question: Why must cells divide as they grow larger?
options: Surface area can't keep up with volume
